title: Integer Iguanas
short_description: Hop Iggy the iguana along a number line — forwards, backwards, and straight past zero into the negatives — to land exactly on the answer.
description: Iggy the iguana starts on a number line and gets a move: '+5', '−7', maybe two hops in a row. Your job is to work out where he lands and tap that spot — the trick being that the line runs both ways, so subtracting past zero drops you into the negative numbers. It's a hands-on way to build integer sense: reading a number line, adding and subtracting, and understanding what below-zero really means. Grade bands ramp from a friendly 0–10 line with single hops, to a −10…10 line that crosses zero, to a −15…15 line with two-step moves. Correct landings and streaks earn bonus points. A free online number line, integers and negative numbers game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap the spot on the number line where the iguana lands.
